message:
Register collections command and list enhancements in CLI

Updates the CLI entry point to expose the new collections command and
enhanced list options to users. This commit wires up the implementations
from the previous two commits.

## Changes to index.ts

### New Import
```typescript
import { collectionsCommand, collectionShowCommand } from "./commands/collections";
```

### Enhanced list command options
Added two new options to the existing list command:
```typescript
.option("--mine", "List only your personal prompts")
.option("--saved", "List only your saved prompts")
```

These flags enable premium users to filter the prompt list to show only
their own prompts or saved/bookmarked prompts respectively.

### New collections command
```typescript
cli
  .command("collections [name]", "List or show user collections (premium)")
  .option("--add <prompt-id>", "Add prompt to collection")
  .option("--json", "Output JSON")
  .action((name, options) => {
    if (name) {
      return collectionShowCommand(name, options);
    }
    return collectionsCommand(options);
  });
```

The command uses an optional positional argument `[name]` to determine behavior:
- No name: Lists all collections (collectionsCommand)
- With name: Shows collection details or adds prompt (collectionShowCommand)

The `--add` flag triggers the add-to-collection flow when a name is provided.

## Command Summary

After this change, users have access to:

```bash
# List commands (existing + enhanced)
jfp list                    # All public prompts
jfp list --mine            # User's own prompts (premium)
jfp list --saved           # User's saved prompts (premium)
jfp list --mine --category ideation  # Combine with filters

# Collections commands (new)
jfp collections                              # List all collections
jfp collections "My Favorites"               # Show collection
jfp collections "My Favorites" --add idea-wizard  # Add to collection
```

## Placement in CLI

The collections command is registered after the notes and save commands,
grouping all premium/personal features together in the command list.
This maintains logical organization:
1. Core prompt commands (list, search, show, etc.)
2. Installation commands (install, uninstall, skills)
3. Premium commands (login, logout, notes, save, collections)
4. Utility commands (interactive, about, doctor)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
